policy.selectionRule: Promote only reproducible, user-visible drift that is likely to recur.
promotionChecklist: The drift affects what a beginner sees first (identity, trust, key features, environment, or owner themes)
The public URL is stable enough to probe again
The expectation can be written against durable fields instead of marketing copy
The case is likely to recur for similar repositories
probeProtocol: repo candidates default to fresh-first probing when GitHub quota allows
owner candidates default to cache-friendly probing and only force refresh right after heuristic changes
when authenticated GitHub quota is exhausted, keep candidates in this backlog instead of writing flaky live expectations
promote to scripts/analysis-regression-cases.json only after the candidate is re-probed successfully

candidates.title: Low-signal public repo for identity/points fallback
candidates.selectionHint: README summary, summary.keyFeatures, and identity.header.points are all weak enough that the Result header risks looking empty.
candidates.notes: Landed via scripts/analysis-regression-cases.json as repo-low-signal-meta. This repo currently exercises the README-based low-signal fallback without needing synthetic fixtures.

candidates.title: SDK + demo app boundary repo
candidates.selectionHint: A repo where libraries/packages coexist with demo or example apps and projectType/consumptionMode can drift.
candidates.notes: Covered by the live regression case repo-sdk-demo-supabase-js. It keeps the SDK/demos boundary on a real public monorepo package.

candidates.title: Large repo where trust summary feels too dense or too vague
candidates.selectionHint: A real partial/limited result where trustSummary.reasons/omissions/basedOn need better prioritization.
candidates.notes: Covered by the live regression case repo-trust-density-n8n. Its limited-mode trust summary already locks headline/reasons/basedOn against a real large public monorepo.

candidates.title: Owner portfolio with theme noise or category drift
candidates.selectionHint: An owner where keyThemes or featured/beginner repo picks still feel stack-token-heavy or semantically noisy.
candidates.notes: Covered by the live owner regression case owner-theme-supabase, including theme-noise filtering around database topic tokens.

candidates.title: Observe rate-limit countdown longer than one hour
candidates.selectionHint: Keep raw retryAfterSeconds behavior unchanged until a real >=3600s countdown is observed.
candidates.notes: Do not add a time-unit UI contract from backend guesses; wait for a real long countdown sample.
